name: undaunted-courage
description: Stephen Ambrose's Undaunted Courage — expedition history toolkit exploring the Lewis and Clark Expedition, Meriwether Lewis's leadership, and the opening of the American West
useCase: Understand the Lewis and Clark Expedition, learn from Lewis's leadership, explore Native American encounters, and analyze the tragic aftermath of great achievement
knownRisks: Ambrose's heroic narrative style may not engage readers who prefer more analytical history
The book focuses heavily on Lewis's perspective — Clark and other Corps members are less developed
Some historians have criticized Ambrose's portrayal of Native Americans as insufficiently critical
